tweets.full_text:
複数エージェントを同時にバックグラウンドで動かせるAIコーディングツール、使っていますか？

Claude Codeの最新アップデートは
・数十〜数百のエージェントを連携する動的ワークフロー管理
・高負荷タスク向けに「/effort xhigh」モードをデフォルト化
・高速モードは2.5倍速でコスト半減を実現

まだX上の投稿はほぼ皆無。
でもこれ、逆に言うと「先行者優位」です。

シェルコマンドを`claude agents`でバックグラウンド実行できるから、CI/CD連携や自動化の幅が広がる。
プラグインは必要に応じて手動管理で依存関係も安心。

複雑な大規模プロジェクトの自動化を始めるなら、今が絶好のタイミングですよ。

【デングくんの冒険⚡ 第89話】

「AIを使う側へ」

Claude CODEを触って思った。

これ――
👉“作業”が激減する。
✔ メール整理
✔ データ処理
✔ SNS運用
✔ アプリ試作
✔ 定型業務

今まで
“時間を奪っていた作業”を
AIが代わりにやり始めている。
すると何が起きるか。
👉人間は“考える時間”が増える。
つまり――
✔ AI
✔ 半導体
✔ エネルギー
✔ ロボティクス
✔ DX/GX

未来を研究する時間が生まれる。
これから重要なのは、
「AIに仕事を奪われる」
じゃない。
👉AIを使いこなす側になれるか。

未来は、
“使った人”から変わり始めるデング⚡

#ClaudeCode #AI活用 #生成AI #DX #GX #半導体 #自動化 #未来技術 #企業公式 #デングくん

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code dynamic workflow moves the bottleneck from prompts to repo rules.

Do not ask if it can run 20 agents.
Ask if 20 outputs can land in one clean PR.

Check:
- scratchpad per agent
- files changed
- tests run
- no-touch files

If those are fuzzy, you scaled cleanup.

tweets.full_text:
One company spent $500M on Claude tokens last month.

A single client. One month. Half a billion dollars in API calls.

Salesforce committed $300M annually. Uber's engineers are burning through Claude Code at a pace that would make a CFO flinch. And the bill only scales up from here.

The entire enterprise AI market is converging on the same model: pay per question, pay per agent call, pay per workflow. The more you use it, the more it costs. The more it works, the worse the bill.

So what happens? Teams throttle usage. Finance flags the spend. The AI that was supposed to drive efficiency becomes a line item someone has to justify every quarter.

More powerful models. More expensive tokens. More unpredictable costs. Every enterprise CFO doing the same math: "What did we actually get for this?"

We built NexDiscovery for exactly this moment.

On-prem. No cloud dependency. No token meter. No vendor deciding what your AI budget should be.

NexDiscovery runs inside your environment, on your infrastructure. It searches your enterprise data for hidden revenue, cost savings, and risk signals. Autonomously. Continuously. Without anyone needing to ask.

We call it Google for margin.

The value was already in your data. Unused pipeline capacity. Misallocated effort. Expansion signals buried in usage patterns. Churn forming in accounts no one was watching.

The rest of the market charges you to ask questions. We find the answers before the question exists.

While everyone debates pricing tiers and usage caps, we deploy in 30 days and the platform pays for itself before the first invoice.

Every enterprise that just saw what $500M in token spend looks like is asking the same question: Is there another way?

There is. It runs on your servers. It has no meter. And it was built for this exact moment.

tweets.full_text:
Claude Code vs Codex vs Grok Build: 
Who builds the best Windows 95 OS in HTML?

I benchmarked them live on speed, lines of code, file size… and actual usability.

Claude: Fastest (384 lines, 14KB) but basic
Grok: Took longer… and built a monster (full Explorer, Paint, interactive Grok terminal, browser, Control Panel, dial-up to AOL, shutdown & more)
Codex/GPT-5: Didn’t quite make it

Out-of-the-box winner? Grok. By a mile. 👑

Full breakdown + results inside 👀

user.name: Dewi

tweets.full_text:
Three things just converged and most people haven't connected the dots yet:

1. Claude Code can now autonomously spawn and orchestrate hundreds of subagents in a single session.
2. Enterprise AI adoption is consolidating around centrally-managed shared agents, not individual setup.
3. The personal AI stack (OC + Hermes + GBrain) gives individuals the same orchestration capability.

The pattern is the same at every scale: agents that have memory, tools, permissions, and recovery. 

The era of "chat with a model" is over. The era of "give intent, get leverage" has started.

tweets.full_text:
agent 的上下文窗口是有限的，compact 之后很多决策细节就丢了 
但其实 claude code 的每一次对话、subagent、workflow 都以 jsonl 的格式保存下来了，信息就在那里，但 agent 没有工具去翻
看到 claude 的 dynamic-workflow 的时候意识到：agent 已经会写脚本来编排工作了，那为什么不能写脚本来检索自己的历史？
所以想到不设计 DSL，不做固定 API，直接让 agent 写 JS 查 SQLite
核心原则就是：code as query language
欢迎大家上手来试试看！单论好玩程度的话，我觉得它已经可以作为一个类似于网易云音乐周听歌记录的东西了，有数据就能有分析（
Repo：https://t.co/WB3ynJKHt9
